candidates.item: Previously, Ms. Zhan was a portfolio manager and managing director at GMO, a Boston-based global investment leader (AUM $68B), from 2002 to 2011, where she was recognized for the firm's successful Greater China equity research and emerging market portfolio management.
candidates.why_interesting: The founder's pre-Beyond career is a genuine credential surprise: GMO is Jeremy Grantham's famously contrarian, bubble-calling firm — not a typical feeder school for boutique real-estate alt managers. A CFA who ran Greater China emerging-market equity at GMO before pivoting to US CRE is a highly unusual origin story that gives the firm a distinct intellectual pedigree.
candidates.recency_note: Bio page live as of April 2026; tenure dates (2002–2011) are historical and stable.

candidates.item: With Three-Year Revenue Growth of 192 Percent, Beyond Ranks No. 2,651 and No. 161 in Financial Services Among America's Fastest-Growing Private Companies.
candidates.why_interesting: 192% three-year revenue growth for a ~$350M AUM alternative investment firm founded in 2020 is a concrete, third-party-verified performance number — and they also made the 2025 Inc. 5000 (No. 2,888, 143% growth), so two consecutive appearances confirm sustained trajectory, not a one-year spike.
candidates.recency_note: Confirmed by both 2024 and 2025 Inc. 5000 listings.

candidates.item: Beyond Global Management is an alternative investment management firm focused on private credit collateralized by real estate assets. Founded in 2020, the firm is led by an executive team with over 100 years of combined investment experience across market cycles.
candidates.why_interesting: Founded in 2020 — the pandemic year — and hit Inc. 5000 twice in four years. The launch timing is a genuine 'bet against consensus' story that aligns with Jenny Zhan's stated distressed-asset contrarian strategy.

candidates.item: Beyond Paradise 1 — I-956F approved, actively under construction, hundreds of jobs already created, and structured with defined exit terms.
candidates.why_interesting: Running an active EB-5 project on Hawaii's Big Island (Kailua-Kona, rural TEA) with both project-level federal approval AND first I-526E petition already approved is a notable operational milestone in the EB-5 space, where most projects stall at one or the other. The rural Hawaii location is also geographically eye-catching — not the usual Texas or Southeast development.
candidates.recency_note: Event page dated March 29, 2026; construction active as of that date.

candidates.item: Jenny Zhan is staking bold capital on distressed commercial real estate assets at a time when most institutional players are tightening their exposure — she calls it a '1-2-3 punch' (pandemic shock, rate surge, construction cost spike) and is doubling down.
candidates.why_interesting: This is a concrete, blunt contrarian stance publicly stated to GlobeSt — not a vague 'value-add' pitch. The specific 'doubling down while institutions tighten' framing is usably distinctive and attributable to a named GlobeSt feature (August 2025), making it a real editorial quote rather than self-marketing copy.

candidates.item: From 2001–2016 worked at PwC China and Singapore as Managing Director, advising 50+ Fortune 500 multinationals and large corporations across different markets. From 2017–2019, Director at Good Hope Investment (CreditEase subsidiary), leading US business development, capital raising, and private fund operations.
candidates.why_interesting: Jason Ye's unusual career arc — 15 years as a Big Four MD advising Fortune 500 multinationals across Asia, then pivoting into cross-border EB-5 fund operations, then joining a boutique US real-estate alt manager — is a specific résumé trajectory that explains why Beyond punches above its $350M AUM weight in cross-border deal origination and Asian LP relationships.
candidates.recency_note: First-party bio page; career dates are historical and stable.

candidates.item: I am representing Beyond Global Management to attend Global Alts Asia 2024 from November 11-13 at Marina Bay Sands, Singapore!
candidates.why_interesting: Jason Ye personally representing a sub-$400M boutique at Global Alts Asia — an event drawing $20T+ in represented assets — signals active, senior-level effort to raise capital from Asian institutional allocators, not just relying on the US-China retail HNW base. It's a specific, verifiable activity (LinkedIn post) rather than a generic claim.

candidates.item: Yet again, Beyond International has maintained a 100% on-time issuance of dividends for its various funds, including those for projects in California, Florida, and other states.
candidates.why_interesting: A 100% on-time dividend record across multiple funds and states is a specific operational claim — rare enough in private real estate alts that it's worth flagging. It's not just a slogan: the news page frames it as a repeated track record, implying it has held through rate hikes and pandemic disruption.
candidates.recency_note: Cited on news page; exact date of most recent confirmation unclear — treat as potentially dated.
